title: Move Application.get_env out of `use Oban.Worker`
prompt: The worker in `test_fixtures/compile_time_queue_worker.ex` calls `Application.get_env/2` inside its `use Oban.Worker` options. Because `use` is a macro, these values freeze at compile time — test and prod configurations collide. Rewrite the module: the `use Oban.Worker` line should specify a static queue (`:exports`) and max_attempts (3). Move the dynamic lookup into a new `enqueue/1` helper that reads `Application.get_env/3` at runtime and passes `queue:` and `max_attempts:` as options to `new/2`. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/export_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Requires understanding that `use` runs at compile time and that runtime options live on `new/2`. The docs warn about this explicitly but Sonnet often reproduces the pattern verbatim.
research_citation: Oban.Worker HexDocs — 'Like all use macros, options are defined at compile time. Avoid using Application.get_env/2 to define worker options.'
